backlog.title: Theme-matched tabs (§17) & deferred preview (§18)
backlog.description: Deliver the two new capabilities added by PRD revision 002 as independent tracks against the already-shipped plan-001 implementation (16 tests green). Track A (§17): the picker can render its items through the theme's own window-status-current-format / window-status-format so it looks like the user's window tabs under any theme, gated by @livepicker-tab-style (plain default). Track B (§18): typing and navigation redraw the status synchronously but defer the live preview to a background, supersedeable run-shell -b job so a keystroke never waits on link-window/select-window, gated by @livepicker-preview-defer (on default). Neither feature fires client-session-changed or touches the saved-state contract; both tear down via clear_all_state. Build target: tmux 3.6b (floor 3.0).

milestones.title: §17 Theme-matched tabs (window-status format reuse)
milestones.description: Add the window-status tab-style path: activation resolves the theme's two window-status formats against a sentinel window and caches the rendered templates; the renderer emits them with the session name swapped in, falling back to plain on any failure. Self-contained: the feature is off by default (@livepicker-tab-style plain), so the existing plain path is untouched.

tasks.title: options.sh + state.sh: add tab-style accessor and template-cache state keys
tasks.description: Add opt_tab_style() to options.sh and the two template-cache runtime state keys (@livepicker-tab-current-tmpl, @livepicker-tab-inactive-tmpl) to state.sh, wiring them into clear_all_state via _STATE_RUNTIME_KEYS. These are the foundation the sentinel resolution (T2) writes and the renderer (T3) reads.

subtasks.title: Add opt_tab_style() accessor and STATE_TAB_CURRENT_TMPL / STATE_TAB_INACTIVE_TMPL state keys
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: codebase_state.md §1 documents the options.sh accessor pattern: each opt_<name>() is a single-line `{ get_opt "@livepicker-<name>" "<default>"; }` body after opt_status_format_index (line 44). A Level-4 cross-check grep expects exactly one `get_opt "@livepicker-<suffix>" "<default>"` per option, so follow the single-line format exactly. state.sh §2 documents: runtime STATE_* constants live at lines 31-36; clear_all_state (lines 139-164) iterates the _STATE_RUNTIME_KEYS space-list (line 49) to `-gu` each — any new runtime key NOT added there LEAKS across picker sessions (system_context.md §3 Q4). PRD §11 default for @livepicker-tab-style is `plain` (NOT 'tabs'/'off' — scouts speculated; the PRD table is authoritative). The template keys are RUNTIME (resolved at activation, cleared on exit), NOT config mirrors (system_context.md §3 Q1).
2. INPUT: The existing scripts/options.sh (get_opt helper at line 15; accessor block lines 26-44) and scripts/state.sh (STATE_* readonly block lines 31-36; _STATE_RUNTIME_KEYS at line 49; set_state/get_state helpers lines 63-75). No new dependency.
3. LOGIC: In options.sh, add after line 44: `opt_tab_style() { get_opt "@livepicker-tab-style" "plain"; }` mirroring the exact single-line pattern. In state.sh, add two readonly constants in the runtime STATE_* block (between lines 36 and 38): `readonly STATE_TAB_CURRENT_TMPL="@livepicker-tab-current-tmpl"` and `readonly STATE_TAB_INACTIVE_TMPL="@livepicker-tab-inactive-tmpl"`. Then append `$STATE_TAB_CURRENT_TMPL $STATE_TAB_INACTIVE_TMPL` to the _STATE_RUNTIME_KEYS string at line 49 so clear_all_state unsets them. Do NOT add these to the ORIG_* saved-state contract — they are picker-internal runtime state, not originals to restore. Verify the empty value is a real `set-option -g @x ""` (set-empty), not unset, so the renderer can distinguish 'not resolved yet' (→ plain fallback) from 'resolved empty'.
4. OUTPUT: opt_tab_style() returns 'plain' or 'window-status'. STATE_TAB_CURRENT_TMPL and STATE_TAB_INACTIVE_TMPL are readable via get_state and clearable via clear_all_state. Consumed by P1.M1.T2.S1 (writes them) and P1.M1.T3.S1 (reads them).
5. DOCS: none — no user-facing/config/API surface change beyond the option row itself (which the Mode-B docs task P1.M3.T3.S1 covers in README). This subtask adds an internal accessor and internal state keys.

tasks.title: livepicker.sh: sentinel window + format resolution + cache
tasks.description: At activation, when opt_tab_style == window-status: create a short-lived hidden sentinel window, resolve both window-status formats via display-message against it, cache the two rendered templates into the T1 state keys, then kill the sentinel. Runs once at activation (no per-keystroke display-message). Guards every step so a styling miss never prevents the picker from opening.

subtasks.title: Sentinel-window format resolution and template caching in activate_main
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: external_tmux_behavior.md Q1 confirms `display-message -p -t <target> "$option_value"` expands the full #{...} tree including #{E:@user_option} re-expansion and #W — pass the OPTION VALUE (via `show-options -gwv window-status-current-format`), NOT the literal `#{window_status_current_format}` (no such format variable exists). Q2 confirms #() status-command stdout is NOT re-parsed for #{...} — only #[...] styles apply — which is exactly why pre-resolution is necessary. Q3 documents the sentinel pattern: create a dedicated HIDDEN 2-window session (anchor + sentinel), so the sentinel is non-active → clean #F (empty), #{window_panes}=1, every *_flag=0 (Q4). Make the session name unique (PID+epoch) to avoid collision. Kill the session after resolution. codebase_state.md §3 documents the activation flow: the insertion point is between the first-preview if-block (~line 326) and `set_state MODE on` (~line 327), so a sentinel-resolution failure can still roll back via the existing L2-fix pattern (restore cancel). tab-style plain skips this entirely.
2. INPUT: opt_tab_style() from P1.M1.T1.S1. STATE_TAB_CURRENT_TMPL / STATE_TAB_INACTIVE_TMPL state keys from P1.M1.T1.S1. The existing activate_main() function in scripts/livepicker.sh (saves originals at STEP 2, builds list at T2, grows status at T3, builds key table at T4, first preview + mode-on at T5 ~lines 292-330). The CURRENT_DIR variable (plugin root, available in livepicker.sh).
3. LOGIC: Implement a helper, e.g. `_lp_resolve_tab_templates()`, called in activate_main between the first-preview block and mode-on, gated on `[ "$(opt_tab_style)" = "window-status" ]`. Steps: (a) Read both format option values: `cur_fmt="$(tmux show-options -gwv window-status-current-format)"` and `reg_fmt="$(tmux show-options -gwv window-status-format)"`. If EITHER is empty, leave both cache keys empty and return 0 (renderer falls back to plain). (b) Create a unique hidden session: `sent_sess="__lp_sent_$$.$(date +%s)"`; `tmux new-session -d -s "$sent_sess" -n __lp_anchor__`; `tmux new-window -d -t "$sent_sess" -n __lp_tab__` (the sentinel is window 1, non-active → clean #F). (c) Resolve: `cur_tpl="$(tmux display-message -p -t "$sent_sess:__lp_tab__" "$cur_fmt")"` and `reg_tpl="$(tmux display-message -p -t "$sent_sess:__lp_tab__" "$reg_fmt")"`. (d) Kill the sentinel: `tmux kill-session -t "$sent_sess" 2>/dev/null || true`. (e) If EITHER resolved template is empty, OR still contains an unexpanded `#{` (a malformed theme that didn't use #{E:...}), leave both cache keys empty (plain fallback) and return 0. (f) Otherwise cache: `set_state "$STATE_TAB_CURRENT_TMPL" "$cur_tpl"` and `set_state "$STATE_TAB_INACTIVE_TMPL" "$reg_tpl"`. GUARD: every tmux call is best-effort (`2>/dev/null || true`); a failure at ANY step leaves cache keys empty and continues activation — the picker must NEVER fail to open over a styling miss (PRD §17 fallback, §16 window-status hijack fragility). The placeholder window name `__lp_tab__` is baked into the resolved templates by display-message; the renderer (T3) swaps it → each session name.
4. OUTPUT: @livepicker-tab-current-tmpl and @livepicker-tab-inactive-tmpl are populated with concrete #[...] styled templates (with `__lp_tab__` as the name placeholder), OR both empty (→ plain fallback). Consumed by P1.M1.T3.S1 (renderer reads them and swaps the placeholder).
5. DOCS: none — internal activation step with no user-facing surface change.

tasks.title: renderer.sh: window-status render path with plain fallback
tasks.description: In render(), add a window-status render branch: when opt_tab_style == window-status AND both template cache keys are non-empty, render each item through the cached template (current for highlighted, inactive for others) with the placeholder name swapped to the session name, joined by window-status-separator. Fall back to the existing plain path verbatim on any missing/empty/failure. Keep the renderer pure and fast (option reads + string replace only; NO display-message here).

subtasks.title: Window-status render branch with placeholder swap and plain fallback
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: codebase_state.md §4 documents render() fully (lines 47-131): it reads STATE_LIST/FILTER/INDEX + options, builds the filtered list via lp_build_filtered (filter.sh), highlights filtered[$cidx] with #[fg=$HFG,bg=$HBG], escapes #→## in names/filters, and prints one line with printf '%s' (NO trailing newline — multi-line stdout renders only the last line). The window-status branch point is right after the option reads (~line 60), before the `out=""` list-build block. external_tmux_behavior.md Q2 confirms #() stdout IS parsed for #[...] style directives — so the cached #[...]-styled templates render correctly when emitted from the renderer. The placeholder window name (`__lp_tab__` from T2) is baked into the templates; swap it → each session name.
2. INPUT: opt_tab_style() from P1.M1.T1.S1. STATE_TAB_CURRENT_TMPL / STATE_TAB_INACTIVE_TMPL from P1.M1.T2.S1 (populated at activation, or empty → fallback). The existing render() function in scripts/renderer.sh with its plain path (the highlight/filter/escape logic). lp_build_filtered from filter.sh (the shared filter). window-status-separator option (read via `tmux show-options -gwv window-status-separator`).
3. LOGIC: In render(), after the option reads and the SHOW_COUNT case (~line 60) but BEFORE the existing plain `out=""` block: branch on `[ "$(opt_tab_style)" = "window-status" ]`. Read both templates: `cur_tpl="$(get_state "$STATE_TAB_CURRENT_TMPL" "")"` and `reg_tpl="$(get_state "$STATE_TAB_INACTIVE_TMPL" "")"`. If EITHER is empty → fall through to the existing plain path verbatim (do NOT duplicate or branch the plain path — just don't enter the window-status block). If BOTH non-empty: build the filtered list the SAME way as plain (mapfile from lp_build_filtered), read the separator `sep="$(tmux show-options -gwv window-status-separator)"` (default to a space if empty), then for each filtered item at index i: pick `tpl="$cur_tpl"` if i==cidx else `tpl="$reg_tpl"`; swap the placeholder: `seg="${tpl//__lp_tab__/${filtered[$i]}}"`; join segments with the separator (NOT a plain space). Handle # escaping on the swapped-in session name: if a session name contains #, it must be doubled (##) so tmux treats it literally — apply the existing `${name//\#/##}` escape to the name BEFORE the substitution, or use a safe substitution. Edge cases: a session name equal to the placeholder string must not corrupt the template (the unique sentinel name makes collision near-impossible); a session name containing # must not break styling. Honor status-justify implicitly (tmux applies it to the whole status line; the renderer only emits the joined string). Keep the SHOW_COUNT query suffix logic — append it after the joined tabs (reuse the plain path's suffix, or omit in window-status mode if it doesn't fit the tab aesthetic; mirror the plain path for consistency). The renderer must stay PURE (no mutations) and FAST (no display-message; option reads + bash string ops only). Preserve the crash guard: the whole render() is still wrapped in `render || printf '#[fg=red]livepicker: renderer error#[default]'`.
4. OUTPUT: renderer.sh emits the window-status-styled list (theme-matched tabs) when tab-style is window-status and templates are cached; otherwise emits the unchanged plain path. This is the user-visible picker appearance. Validated by P1.M3.T2.S1.
5. DOCS: none — renderer output is internal status-line content (the option itself is documented in the Mode-B README task P1.M3.T3.S1).

milestones.title: §18 Deferred preview (interaction-first responsiveness)
milestones.description: Rework the input→preview data flow: the typing and nav paths stop calling preview.sh inline and instead schedule a background, supersedeable preview; preview.sh gains a sequence guard so a late job whose target was superseded is a no-op. Default-on; @livepicker-preview-defer off restores the synchronous path (current behavior) for diagnosis.

tasks.title: options.sh + state.sh: add preview-defer accessor and sequence/target state keys
tasks.description: Add opt_preview_defer() to options.sh and the @livepicker-preview-seq counter + @livepicker-preview-target runtime state keys to state.sh, wiring them into clear_all_state. These back the supersede mechanism: the handler bumps the seq when it fires a preview; the background job captures the seq at fire time and preview.sh re-reads it before mutating.

subtasks.title: Add opt_preview_defer() accessor and STATE_PREVIEW_SEQ / STATE_PREVIEW_TARGET state keys
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: codebase_state.md §1/§2 document the patterns (same as P1.M1.T1.S1). external_tmux_behavior.md Q6 documents the supersede pattern: a monotonic sequence number in a @livepicker-* option, captured at fire time, re-checked by the background job before mutating. system_context.md §3 Q3 confirms preview-defer default is `on` and the seq guard is active when on; `off` restores synchronous. The seq must be initialized to 0 at activation (in livepicker.sh, alongside the other state inits).
2. INPUT: scripts/options.sh (get_opt, accessor block) and scripts/state.sh (STATE_* block, _STATE_RUNTIME_KEYS, set_state/get_state). No new dependency.
3. LOGIC: In options.sh add after opt_tab_style(): `opt_preview_defer() { get_opt "@livepicker-preview-defer" "on"; }`. In state.sh add two readonly constants: `readonly STATE_PREVIEW_SEQ="@livepicker-preview-seq"` and `readonly STATE_PREVIEW_TARGET="@livepicker-preview-target"`, then append `$STATE_PREVIEW_SEQ $STATE_PREVIEW_TARGET` to _STATE_RUNTIME_KEYS (line 49) so clear_all_state clears them. Also: in livepicker.sh activate_main, during the state-init region (STEP 2 / the T2 list-build area, ~line 82 where STATE_LINKED_ID is initialized to empty), add `set_state "$STATE_PREVIEW_SEQ" "0"` to initialize the counter to 0 at activation.
4. OUTPUT: opt_preview_defer() returns 'on' or 'off'. STATE_PREVIEW_SEQ (an incrementing integer) and STATE_PREVIEW_TARGET (a session/window token) are readable/writable via set_state/get_state and cleared on exit. Consumed by P1.M2.T2.S1 (seq guard in preview.sh) and P1.M2.T3.S1 (bump + fire in input-handler.sh).
5. DOCS: none — internal accessor and state keys (the option row is covered by the Mode-B README task P1.M3.T3.S1).

tasks.title: preview.sh: supersede/sequence guard + background-safe entry
tasks.description: Add an optional second arg preview.sh <session> <expected_seq>. When provided, read the live @livepicker-preview-seq at the top of preview_main and if it differs, return 0 without unlinking/linking/selecting (a stale job must never clobber the current link). When called with one arg (activation first-preview and preview-defer off synchronous path), behave exactly as today. Also re-read linked-id inside the body so a job that raced a newer link does not unlink the newer window.

subtasks.title: Add expected_seq arg and supersede guard to preview_main
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: codebase_state.md §5 documents preview_main() fully (lines 75-166): arg parsing is `local S="${1:-}"` at line 76; the preview-mode gate is at line 86; the self-session guard at lines 104-113; src_id resolution at lines 119-129; duplicate guard at lines 134-138; unlink-previous at lines 143-145; link+select+track at lines 150-164. external_tmux_behavior.md Q5 confirms run-shell -b is NOT cancellable by id — a late job MUST be a no-op via the seq guard. Q6 documents the exact guard placement: check at the TOP before any mutating work, and optionally re-check before the final select to close the read→mutate race. CRITICAL: if the picker exits (cancel/confirm) while a -b job is mid-flight, the late job will still run and call back into tmux — without the seq guard it would unlink/link AFTER teardown, clobbering restored state.
2. INPUT: STATE_PREVIEW_SEQ from P1.M2.T1.S1. The existing preview_main() in scripts/preview.sh. get_state helper from state.sh. STATE_LINKED_ID (existing, line 31-36).
3. LOGIC: At line 76, change arg parsing to `local S="${1:-}" expected_seq="${2:-}"`. Immediately after (before the preview-mode gate at line 86), add the supersede guard: `if [ -n "$expected_seq" ]; then local cur_seq; cur_seq="$(get_state "$STATE_PREVIEW_SEQ" "0")"; [ "$cur_seq" != "$expected_seq" ] && return 0; fi`. This means: when called with a seq (the deferred background path), if the live seq has advanced (a newer keystroke fired a newer preview), this job is stale → return 0 WITHOUT touching any window. When called with one arg (no seq — the activation first-preview and the preview-defer off synchronous path), the guard is skipped and behavior is exactly as today. ADDITIONALLY: re-read linked_id inside the link/select body right before the unlink step (~line 143), not just once at the top (line 83), so a job that raced a newer link does not unlink the newer window: `linked_id="$(get_state "$STATE_LINKED_ID" "")"` re-read before the `if [ -n "$linked_id" ]; then unlink-window ...` block. Optionally add a second seq re-check right before the final select-window (~line 158) to close the remaining read→mutate window. The existing link/unlink/select core logic is UNCHANGED — this only adds prechecks.
4. OUTPUT: preview.sh accepts `preview.sh <session> [expected_seq]`. With a seq, it is supersede-safe (late jobs no-op). With one arg, unchanged. Consumed by P1.M2.T3.S1 (fires background jobs with the seq).
5. DOCS: none — internal script interface change.

tasks.title: input-handler.sh: defer preview on type/backspace/nav when preview-defer is on
tasks.description: When opt_preview_defer == on (default): replace the inline _lp_sync_preview_to_top_match call in type/backspace/cancel-clear and the inline preview.sh call in next-session/prev-session with a deferred schedule (increment seq, set target, run-shell -b). The synchronous work on these paths becomes ONLY: update filter/index state + refresh-client -S. When opt_preview_defer == off, call preview.sh inline as today (legacy path). Confirm branch is unchanged (already reads authoritative state).

subtasks.title: Centralize deferred-preview fire helper and rewire type/backspace/nav/cancel-clear call sites
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: codebase_state.md §6 documents the call sites: _lp_sync_preview_to_top_match (lines 130-138) is called inline at type (~line 172), backspace (~line 210), and cancel-clear (~line 400); next-session calls `preview.sh "$target"` directly at ~line 246; prev-session at ~line 265. The confirm branch (~lines 281-379) is ALREADY compliant — it resolves target from authoritative @livepicker-filter/@livepicker-index and never calls preview.sh, so confirm already does not block on a preview (PRD §18 contract point 4). external_tmux_behavior.md Q6 provides the reference _lp_fire_preview helper: read+bump STATE_PREVIEW_SEQ, set STATE_PREVIEW_TARGET, then `tmux run-shell -b "$CURRENT_DIR/preview.sh '<target>' '<seq>'"`. Use an ABSOLUTE path (the server's cwd is not the plugin dir); the script's bash shebang is honored under run-shell. Q5 confirms -b is non-blocking and a bare tmux inside the job reaches the same server. PRD §18 contract: typing path does exactly two things synchronously — update filter/index, then refresh-client -S; nav updates index + refresh-client -S immediately, defers preview.
2. INPUT: opt_preview_defer() from P1.M2.T1.S1. STATE_PREVIEW_SEQ / STATE_PREVIEW_TARGET from P1.M2.T1.S1. preview.sh with the expected_seq arg from P1.M2.T2.S1. The existing _lp_sync_preview_to_top_match (lines 130-138) and the four call sites. CURRENT_DIR (available in input-handler.sh). lp_build_filtered (filter.sh — the shared filter, already used by _lp_sync_preview_to_top_match).
3. LOGIC: Add a new helper `_lp_fire_preview()` that takes a target token ($1): reads `seq="$(get_state "$STATE_PREVIEW_SEQ" "0")"`; increments `seq=$((seq+1))`; `set_state "$STATE_PREVIEW_SEQ" "$seq"`; `set_state "$STATE_PREVIEW_TARGET" "$1"`; then fires `tmux run-shell -b "$CURRENT_DIR/preview.sh '$1' '$seq'"` (quote-safe the target; use single quotes inside the double-quoted command so session names with spaces are handled — or escape appropriately). Optionally add a tiny debounce via `run-shell -b -d 80 "$CURRENT_DIR/preview.sh ..."` (PRD §18 'a short debounce may gate the background fire'); if debouncing, the seq re-check in preview.sh (T2) collapses a burst to one trailing fire. Then rewire the call sites CONDITIONALLY on opt_preview_defer: (a) type branch (~line 172): after set_state FILTER + set_state INDEX 0 + refresh-client -S, if `[ "$(opt_preview_defer)" = "on" ]` then `_lp_fire_preview "${top_match}"` (compute top_match via lp_build_filtered, same as _lp_sync_preview_to_top_match does today), else call the existing `_lp_sync_preview_to_top_match` inline (legacy). (b) backspace branch (~line 210): same pattern. (c) cancel-clear branch (~line 400): same pattern (cancel-clear keeps the picker OPEN and clears the query; the preview follows the new top match). (d) next-session (~line 246) and prev-session (~line 265): after set_state INDEX + refresh-client -S, if defer-on then `_lp_fire_preview "$target"`, else `"$CURRENT_DIR/preview.sh" "$target"` inline. GUARD the empty-filtered case: if there is no top match (filtered list empty after a type/backspace), schedule nothing — leave the prior preview as-is (do not fire a preview for a non-existent target). VERIFY: the type→Enter-within-~100ms path lands correctly with NO preview having run (confirm reads authoritative filter/index state and does not depend on the background job). Keep the cancel full-exit branch and confirm branch UNCHANGED.
4. OUTPUT: When @livepicker-preview-defer is on (default), typing/backspace/nav redraw the status synchronously (filter/index + refresh-client -S) and schedule a background supersedeable preview; confirm never blocks on a preview. When off, the legacy synchronous path is restored. Validated by P1.M3.T1.S1.
5. DOCS: none — internal input-flow change (the option is covered by the Mode-B README task P1.M3.T3.S1).

milestones.title: Validation (§15.23 + §15.24) + changeset-level documentation
milestones.description: Feature-specific tests under the existing socket-isolated harness, mirroring the new §15 subsections, then the cross-cutting Mode-B documentation sync (README config table + notes, CHANGELOG entry).

tasks.title: tests/test_responsiveness.sh — §15.23 deferred-preview validation
tasks.description: Assert the §18 responsiveness contract: typing redraws status before any preview work runs; three keystrokes + immediate Enter lands correctly with no preview backlog; a superseded preview is a no-op; navigation moves the highlight before the preview catches up; preview-defer off restores synchronous preview.

subtasks.title: Write test_responsiveness.sh under the socket-isolated harness
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: test_harness.md documents the full harness contract: tests are SOURCED by run.sh (never executed directly); define test_* functions only; run.sh calls setup_test "lp-$$-<name>" per test (fresh isolated socket via PATH shim); teardown_test auto-cleans. set -u is inherited. Assertion API: fail/pass/assert_eq/assert_contains + inline case+fail for negatives. Responsiveness tests need attach_test_client (for refresh-client -S / switch-client). Mirror test_functional.sh::test_activate_grows_status lifecycle. codebase_state.md §6 documents the seq/target state keys and the _lp_fire_preview mechanism. external_tmux_behavior.md Q5/Q7 note: on a client-less detached socket, refresh-client -S is a no-op (guard with 2>/dev/null || true); a real attached client is needed. There is no helper to resize; use bare tmux resize-window if needed. NOTE: a -b job is not cancellable and not instantly observable — the test must account for async timing (the seq advances synchronously at fire time even though the link lags; assert on STATE_PREVIEW_SEQ / STATE_LINKED_ID divergence).
2. INPUT: The deferred-preview implementation from P1.M2.T3.S1 (input-handler.sh _lp_fire_preview, preview.sh seq guard). STATE_PREVIEW_SEQ / STATE_PREVIEW_TARGET / STATE_LINKED_ID state keys. tests/setup_socket.sh (setup_test, attach_test_client, detach_test_client, TEST_DRIVER_SESSION, TEST_FIXTURE_SESSIONS, LIVEPICKER_SCRIPTS). tests/helpers.sh (fail, assert_eq, assert_contains). The existing test files as templates (test_functional.sh for activate/attach lifecycle; test_pollution.sh for fixture-install pattern).
3. LOGIC: Create tests/test_responsiveness.sh. Define test_* functions: (a) test_typing_defers_preview: activate the picker (attach_test_client + run livepicker.sh), type one char, assert the renderer output (run renderer.sh or read status-format via display-message) reflects the new query WHILE @livepicker-preview-seq advanced but @livepicker-linked-id has NOT yet changed to a new target (the link lags the status). Instrument: read STATE_PREVIEW_SEQ right after the type action (it advanced synchronously) and assert STATE_LINKED_ID is still the pre-type value (background job hasn't run yet in the test window). (b) test_rapid_type_confirm_no_backlog: set up 3 sessions, type 3 chars rapidly (call input-handler.sh type x3) then immediately confirm (input-handler.sh confirm), assert the client lands on the correct target and at most ONE link-window occurred (count links by diffing STATE_LINKED_ID before/after, or instrument preview.sh with a marker). (c) test_superseded_preview_noop: fire two _lp_fire_preview schedules rapidly for different targets (call input-handler.sh next-session twice fast), then sleep briefly for the background jobs to complete, assert only the LATEST target's window is linked (STATE_LINKED_ID matches the latest target's active window id) and the stale target was never unlinked after the newer link. (d) test_nav_moves_highlight_before_preview: activate, call next-session, immediately read renderer output — assert the highlight moved to the next session (status) even before the preview catches up (STATE_LINKED_ID still old). (e) test_preview_defer_off_synchronous: set @livepicker-preview-defer off, type one char, assert preview ran inline (STATE_LINKED_ID changed synchronously, matching the old behavior). Each test calls attach_test_client before driving livepicker.sh and asserts via the helpers. Use inline case+fail for negative checks (e.g., assert a stale target id is NOT in STATE_LINKED_ID).
4. OUTPUT: tests/test_responsiveness.sh passes under `bash tests/run.sh`, validating PRD §15.23. No production code change.
5. DOCS: none — test file.

tasks.title: tests/test_appearance.sh — §15.24 window-status tab validation
tasks.description: Install representative window-status-format/window-status-current-format/window-status-separator fixtures on the isolated socket (tubular is not loaded there), activate with @livepicker-tab-style window-status, and assert the highlighted item matches the current-format styling and others the inactive-format; assert the inter-item gap matches the separator; assert empty/unresolvable format falls back to plain; assert tab-style plain (default) is unchanged.

subtasks.title: Write test_appearance.sh under the socket-isolated harness
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: test_harness.md documents: appearance tests need NO client (renderer is client-independent — reads state only). Mirror test_functional.sh::test_renderer_escapes_hash_* (seed @livepicker-* options → run $LIVEPICKER_SCRIPTS/renderer.sh → assert_contains on stdout). The tubular theme is NOT loaded on the isolated socket, so install representative window-status-format / window-status-current-format / window-status-separator fixtures directly via tmux set-option -gw (global-window). test_harness.md §4 documents the setup_test-internal precedent (test_functional.sh calls setup_test internally for custom fixtures). codebase_state.md §4 documents renderer.sh's output contract: one line, no trailing newline, #[fg=...,bg=...] segments. external_tmux_behavior.md Q1/Q3/Q4 document the sentinel resolution and what specifiers resolve to. For the window-status path, the sentinel resolution (P1.M1.T2.S1) runs inside livepicker.sh activate — but since the renderer reads the CACHED templates, a test can either (a) drive a full activate with tab-style window-status (needs attach_test_client for the sentinel display-message resolution, OR verify display-message works client-less on the socket), or (b) directly seed STATE_TAB_CURRENT_TMPL / STATE_TAB_INACTIVE_TMPL with known-resolved templates and test the renderer's swap logic in isolation (cleaner, no activate dependency). Use approach (b) for the renderer-output assertions and approach (a) for one end-to-end sentinel-resolution test.
2. INPUT: The window-status render path from P1.M1.T3.S1 (renderer.sh). opt_tab_style() / STATE_TAB_CURRENT_TMPL / STATE_TAB_INACTIVE_TMPL from P1.M1.T1.S1. The sentinel resolution from P1.M1.T2.S1. tests/setup_socket.sh + tests/helpers.sh. The existing test_functional.sh renderer-test idiom as template.
3. LOGIC: Create tests/test_appearance.sh. Define test_* functions: (a) test_window_status_highlight_uses_current_format: seed STATE_LIST with 3 sessions (e.g. alpha beta driver), STATE_FILTER empty, STATE_INDEX 1 (highlight beta). Set window-status-current-format and window-status-format fixtures on the socket (e.g. a simple `#[fg=red,bold]#W#[default]` for current, `#[fg=blue]#W#[default]` for inactive). Set @livepicker-tab-style window-status. EITHER run a full activate (attach_test_client + livepicker.sh) to trigger sentinel resolution and populate the cache keys, OR directly seed STATE_TAB_CURRENT_TMPL / STATE_TAB_INACTIVE_TMPL with the resolved templates (the sentinel name __lp_tab__ baked in — e.g. `#[fg=red,bold]__lp_tab__#[default]`). Run `out="$($LIVEPICKER_SCRIPTS/renderer.sh)"`. assert_contains "$out" the current-format styling for beta and the inactive-format styling for alpha/driver. (b) test_window_status_separator: set window-status-separator to a known string (e.g. '|'); assert the inter-item gap in the renderer output matches it. (c) test_empty_format_falls_back_to_plain: set window-status-current-format to empty (or leave templates unseeded); with tab-style window-status, assert the renderer output matches the plain path (#[fg=$HFG,bg=$HBG] styling, NOT the window-status format). (d) test_tab_style_plain_unchanged: with @livepicker-tab-style plain (default), assert the renderer output is identical to the pre-delta plain behavior (the existing test_functional.sh renderer assertions should still pass). (e) test_sentinel_resolution_end_to_end (optional, needs attach_test_client): set real-ish window-status formats, activate with tab-style window-status, assert the cache keys are populated with #[...]-styled templates containing the __lp_tab__ placeholder, and that killing the sentinel session left no stray __lp_sent_* session.
4. OUTPUT: tests/test_appearance.sh passes under `bash tests/run.sh`, validating PRD §15.24. No production code change.
5. DOCS: none — test file.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation (README.md + CHANGELOG.md)
tasks.description: Mode-B catch-all: update README.md (add the two new options to the configuration table; add short Appearance and Performance notes) and CHANGELOG.md (an entry for both features noting defaults and that they do not change the pollution/restore invariants). Do NOT edit PRD.md (read-only).

subtasks.title: Update README.md config table + notes and CHANGELOG.md entry for both features
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The repo has README.md (11KB, has a configuration table and usage sections) and CHANGELOG.md (6KB, documents invariants and feature additions). Both were written in plan-001 and do NOT yet mention @livepicker-tab-style or @livepicker-preview-defer. system_context.md §2 confirms the PRD §11 defaults: tab-style=plain, preview-defer=on.
2. INPUT: The two implemented features: §17 theme-matched tabs (P1.M1.T3.S1 — @livepicker-tab-style, default plain, window-status mode reuses the theme's window-status formats) and §18 deferred preview (P1.M2.T3.S1 — @livepicker-preview-defer, default on, defers the live preview to a background job so typing/nav stay snappy). The passing tests from P1.M3.T1.S1 and P1.M3.T2.S1.
3. LOGIC: README.md — add two rows to the configuration options table: `@livepicker-tab-style` (default `plain`; `window-status` reuses the theme's window-status-current-format/window-status-format so picker tabs match your window tabs; falls back to plain on failure) and `@livepicker-preview-defer` (default `on`; defers the live preview to a background job so typing and navigation never wait on link-window/select-window; set `off` to restore synchronous preview for diagnosis). Add a short 'Appearance' note (the picker can match your window-status theme via @livepicker-tab-style window-status) and a 'Performance' note (the preview is deferred by default so the status line tracks every keystroke instantly; set @livepicker-preview-defer off for the legacy synchronous path). CHANGELOG.md — add an entry for both features: note they are default-on (preview-defer) / default-off (tab-style), that neither changes the pollution/restore invariants (no client-session-changed while browsing; clear_all_state still tears down everything), and that the existing plain tab-style and synchronous preview paths remain as the default / the off escape hatch. Do NOT edit PRD.md (read-only). Follow the write-tech-docs skill style (no em dashes, no marketing tell-words, concise).
4. OUTPUT: README.md and CHANGELOG.md reflect the two new options and capabilities. A reader can discover and configure both features from the README alone.
5. DOCS: this IS the Mode-B documentation task. It depends on every implementing and test subtask so it runs last and summarizes the complete changeset.
